chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Mutual Inheritance of a Man and a Woman Whom He Marries and Then Divorces During His Illness

text: 5667 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ اَلْحَنَّاطِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ فِي مَرَضِهِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا دَخَلَ بِهَا فَمَاتَ فِي مَرَضِهِ وَرِثَتْهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ بِهَا لَمْ تَرِثْهُ وَ نِكَاحُهُ بَاطِلٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5667 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad Al-Hannat, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who married during his illness.
Imam (as) said: "If he consummated the marriage with her and then died during his illness, she inherits from him. However, if he did not consummate the marriage with her, she does not inherit from him, and the marriage is invalid."
